{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents consistently indicate that heparin works by binding to antithrombin III, which enhances its ability to inactivate clotting factors, particularly factor Xa and thrombin. This mechanism prevents the conversion of fibrinogen to fibrin, which is the final step in clot formation. None of the documents mention heparin preventing the conversion of prothrombin to thrombin, PTC to PTA, or factor VIIa inhibition as the main mechanism of action.", "answer_choice": "A"}
